title: Build for archive posted to podman unix socket fails (insufficient UIDs or GIDs available in user namespace)
body:
### Issue Description

Hi! I'm working with a project that uses a maven plugin to execute a docker build ([fabric8io/docker-maven-plugin](https://dmp.fabric8.io/)) and I'm pointing it at a podman unix socket. The build fails on my machine with:

```
[INFO] --- docker:0.46.0:build (default) @ kroxylicious-app ---
[INFO] Building tar: /home/robeyoun/development/upstream/kroxylicious/kroxylicious-app/target/docker/quay.io/kroxylicious/proxy/0.13.0-SNAPSHOT/tmp/docker-build.tar
[INFO] DOCKER> [quay.io/kroxylicious/proxy:0.13.0-SNAPSHOT] "pxy": Created docker-build.tar in 1 second 
[ERROR] DOCKER> Unable to build image [quay.io/kroxylicious/proxy:0.13.0-SNAPSHOT] : Connection reset by peer [Connection reset by peer]
```

when it tries to POST an image-build to `/run/user/4210516/podman/podman.sock`

I can see in the podman journalctl logs:

```
May 29 10:48:43 robeyoun-thinkpadp16vgen1.rmtnz.csb podman[911361]: @ - - [29/May/2025:10:48:43 +1200] "GET /version HTTP/1.1" 200 1021 "" "Apache-HttpClient/4.5.13 (Java/21.0.4)"
May 29 10:48:44 robeyoun-thinkpadp16vgen1.rmtnz.csb podman[911361]: @ - - [29/May/2025:10:48:44 +1200] "GET /v1.41/images/registry.access.redhat.com%2Fubi9%2Fubi-minimal%3A9.6-1747218906/json HTTP/1.1" 200 3059 "" "Apache-HttpClient/4.5.13 (Java/21.0.4)"
May 29 10:48:44 robeyoun-thinkpadp16vgen1.rmtnz.csb podman[911361]: @ - - [29/May/2025:10:48:44 +1200] "GET /v1.41/images/registry.access.redhat.com%2Fubi9%2Fubi-minimal%3A9.6-1747218906/json HTTP/1.1" 200 3059 "" "Apache-HttpClient/4.5.13 (Java/21.0.4)"
May 29 10:48:44 robeyoun-thinkpadp16vgen1.rmtnz.csb podman[911361]: @ - - [29/May/2025:10:48:44 +1200] "GET /v1.41/images/registry.access.redhat.com%2Fubi9%2Fubi-minimal%3A9.6-1747218906/json HTTP/1.1" 200 3059 "" "Apache-HttpClient/4.5.13 (Java/21.0.4)"
May 29 10:48:44 robeyoun-thinkpadp16vgen1.rmtnz.csb podman[911361]: time="2025-05-29T10:48:44+12:00" level=info msg="Request Failed(Not Found): failed to find image quay.io/kroxylicious/proxy:0.13.0-SNAPSHOT: quay.io/kroxylicious/proxy:0.13.0-SNAPSHOT: No such image"
May 29 10:48:44 robeyoun-thinkpadp16vgen1.rmtnz.csb podman[911361]: @ - - [29/May/2025:10:48:44 +1200] "GET /v1.41/images/quay.io%2Fkroxylicious%2Fproxy%3A0.13.0-SNAPSHOT/json HTTP/1.1" 404 285 "" "Apache-HttpClient/4.5.13 (Java/21.0.4)"
May 29 10:48:44 robeyoun-thinkpadp16vgen1.rmtnz.csb podman[911361]: time="2025-05-29T10:48:44+12:00" level=info msg="Request Failed(Internal Server Error): potentially insufficient UIDs or GIDs available in user namespace (requested 4210516:4210516 for /var/tmp/libpod_builder2552006587/build/proxy.dockerfile): Check /etc/subuid and /etc/subgid if configured locally and run \"podman system migrate\": lchown /var/tmp/libpod_builder2552006587/build/proxy.dockerfile: invalid argument"
May 29 10:48:44 robeyoun-thinkpadp16vgen1.rmtnz.csb podman[911361]: @ - - [29/May/2025:10:48:44 +1200] "POST /v1.41/build?buildargs=%7B%22KROXYLICIOUS_VERSION%22%3A%220.13.0-SNAPSHOT%22%7D&dockerfile=proxy.dockerfile&forcerm=1&nocache=0&squash=0&t=quay.io%2Fkroxylicious%2Fproxy%3A0.13.0-SNAPSHOT HTTP/1.1" 500 385 "" "Apache-HttpClient/4.5.13 (Java/21.0.4)"
May 29 10:48:44 robeyoun-thinkpadp16vgen1.rmtnz.csb podman[911361]: time="2025-05-29T10:48:44+12:00" level=info msg="Request Failed(Internal Server Error): potentially insufficient UIDs or GIDs available in user namespace (requested 4210516:4210516 for /var/tmp/libpod_builder973029959/build/proxy.dockerfile): Check /etc/subuid and /etc/subgid if configured locally and run \"podman system migrate\": lchown /var/tmp/libpod_builder973029959/build/proxy.dockerfile: invalid argument"
May 29 10:48:44 robeyoun-thinkpadp16vgen1.rmtnz.csb podman[911361]: @ - - [29/May/2025:10:48:44 +1200] "POST /v1.41/build?buildargs=%7B%22KROXYLICIOUS_VERSION%22%3A%220.13.0-SNAPSHOT%22%7D&dockerfile=proxy.dockerfile&forcerm=1&nocache=0&squash=0&t=quay.io%2Fkroxylicious%2Fproxy%3A0.13.0-SNAPSHOT HTTP/1.1" 500 383 "" "Apache-HttpClient/4.5.13 (Java/21.0.4)"
May 29 10:48:45 robeyoun-thinkpadp16vgen1.rmtnz.csb podman[911361]: time="2025-05-29T10:48:45+12:00" level=info msg="Request Failed(Internal Server Error): potentially insufficient UIDs or GIDs available in user namespace (requested 4210516:4210516 for /var/tmp/libpod_builder148438572/build/proxy.dockerfile): Check /etc/subuid and /etc/subgid if configured locally and run \"podman system migrate\": lchown /var/tmp/libpod_builder148438572/build/proxy.dockerfile: invalid argument"
May 29 10:48:45 robeyoun-thinkpadp16vgen1.rmtnz.csb podman[911361]: @ - - [29/May/2025:10:48:45 +1200] "POST /v1.41/build?buildargs=%7B%22KROXYLICIOUS_VERSION%22%3A%220.13.0-SNAPSHOT%22%7D&dockerfile=proxy.dockerfile&forcerm=1&nocache=0&squash=0&t=quay.io%2Fkroxylicious%2Fproxy%3A0.13.0-SNAPSHOT HTTP/1.1" 500 383 "" "Apache-HttpClient/4.5.13 (Java/21.0.4)"
May 29 10:48:45 robeyoun-thinkpadp16vgen1.rmtnz.csb podman[911361]: time="2025-05-29T10:48:45+12:00" level=info msg="Request Failed(Internal Server Error): potentially insufficient UIDs or GIDs available in user namespace (requested 4210516:4210516 for /var/tmp/libpod_builder3477251578/build/proxy.dockerfile): Check /etc/subuid and /etc/subgid if configured locally and run \"podman system migrate\": lchown /var/tmp/libpod_builder3477251578/build/proxy.dockerfile: invalid argument"
May 29 10:48:45 robeyoun-thinkpadp16vgen1.rmtnz.csb podman[911361]: @ - - [29/May/2025:10:48:45 +1200] "POST /v1.41/build?buildargs=%7B%22KROXYLICIOUS_VERSION%22%3A%220.13.0-SNAPSHOT%22%7D&dockerfile=proxy.dockerfile&forcerm=1&nocache=0&squash=0&t=quay.io%2Fkroxylicious%2Fproxy%3A0.13.0-SNAPSHOT HTTP/1.1" 500 385 "" "Apache-HttpClient/4.5.13 (Java/21.0.4)"
```

After some playing around with a reproducer I think podman is unhappy that it's receiving a `tar` which contains files owned by my uid. Maybe this is working as intended? Keen to understand the failure in any case.

Thank you!

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1.  ```shell
    #!/bin/bash
    DIR="$(mktemp -d)"
    mkdir "${DIR}/image"
    echo 'FROM scratch' > "${DIR}/image/Containerfile"
    tar -cvf "${DIR}/build.tar" -C "${DIR}/image" .
    curl --unix-socket "$(podman info --format '{{.Host.RemoteSocket.Path}}')" 'http://v1.41/build?dockerfile=Containerfile' --data-binary @${DIR}/build.tar --header 'Content-type: application/x-tar' -v
    ```

### Describe the results you received

Describe the results you received:

```
*   Trying /run/user/4210516/podman/podman.sock:0...
* Connected to v1.41 (/run/user/4210516/podman/podman.sock) port 0
* using HTTP/1.x
> POST /build?dockerfile=Containerfile HTTP/1.1
> Host: v1.41
> User-Agent: curl/8.11.1
> Accept: */*
> Content-type: application/x-tar
> Content-Length: 10240
> 
* upload completely sent off: 10240 bytes
< HTTP/1.1 500 Internal Server Error
< Api-Version: 1.41
< Content-Type: application/json
< Libpod-Api-Version: 5.5.0
< Server: Libpod/5.5.0 (linux)
< X-Reference-Id: 0xc000096000
< Date: Thu, 29 May 2025 01:17:12 GMT
< Content-Length: 377
< 
{"cause":"invalid argument","message":"potentially insufficient UIDs or GIDs available in user namespace (requested 4210516:4210516 for /var/tmp/libpod_builder439104631/build/Containerfile): Check /etc/subuid and /etc/subgid if configured locally and run \"podman system migrate\": lchown /var/tmp/libpod_builder439104631/build/Containerfile: invalid argument","response":500}
* Connection #0 to host v1.41 left intact
```

### Describe the results you expected

the image builds successfully:

for example if I set the owner/group on the contents of the tar to not be my user, like:

```
#!/bin/bash

DIR="$(mktemp -d)"
mkdir "${DIR}/image"
echo 'FROM scratch' > "${DIR}/image/Containerfile"
tar -cvf "${DIR}/build.tar" --owner=10000 --group=10000 -C "${DIR}/image" .
curl --unix-socket "$(podman info --format '{{.Host.RemoteSocket.Path}}')" 'http://v1.41/build?dockerfile=Containerfile' --data-binary @${DIR}/build.tar --header 'Content-type: application/x-tar' -v
```

then the curl succeeds with:

```
*   Trying /run/user/4210516/podman/podman.sock:0...
* Connected to v1.41 (/run/user/4210516/podman/podman.sock) port 0
* using HTTP/1.x
> POST /build?dockerfile=Containerfile HTTP/1.1
> Host: v1.41
> User-Agent: curl/8.11.1
> Accept: */*
> Content-type: application/x-tar
> Content-Length: 10240
> 
* upload completely sent off: 10240 bytes
< HTTP/1.1 200 OK
< Api-Version: 1.41
< Content-Type: application/json
< Libpod-Api-Version: 5.5.0
< Server: Libpod/5.5.0 (linux)
< X-Reference-Id: 0xc000b002e8
< Date: Thu, 29 May 2025 01:20:13 GMT
< Transfer-Encoding: chunked
< 
{"stream":"STEP 1/1: FROM scratch\n"}
{"stream":"COMMIT\n"}
{"stream":"--\u003e 5b1636a4f388\n"}
{"stream":"5b1636a4f38835f271a96a4bb0ab06a590745c9fa0f4fd7233baa5f7ceaaf0f7\n"}
{"aux":{"ID":"sha256:5b1636a4f38835f271a96a4bb0ab06a590745c9fa0f4fd7233baa5f7ceaaf0f7"}}
{"stream":"Successfully built 5b1636a4f388\n"}
* Connection #0 to host v1.41 left intact
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details:

```
cat /etc/subuid
robeyoun:10000:75536
cat /etc/subgid
robeyoun:10000:75536
echo $UID
4210516
uname -r
6.14.6-300.fc42.x86_64
```

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body: My workmate doesn't see this issue, difference being he has a UID of `1000` where I have this enormous one `4210516`.
And now it's started working, and I don't know what's changed. I'll reopen if it happens again

Okay this cropped up again for our team and a workaround that seems to fix is allocating an enormous subuid range that can map my user id (and does not overlap with my userid)

```
$ cat /etc/subuid
robeyoun:4210517:4210517
$ cat /etc/subgid
robeyoun:4210517:4210517
```

followed by a `podman system migrate`
